name: The First National Bank Of Proctor
description_short: Independent community bank in Proctor, Minnesota, serving the area since 1902 with checking, savings, loans, and online banking services.
description_long:
The First National Bank of Proctor is an independent, locally-operated bank headquartered in Proctor, Minnesota, with roots in the community dating back to 1902. As a small regional institution with approximately $25 million in assets and a staff of 8, it operates a single location at 211 Second Street in Proctor while maintaining the operational standards of a modern financial institution.

The bank offers standard consumer banking products including checking and savings accounts, mobile banking access, online banking, debit/cash cards (through Visa/Shazam), safe deposit boxes, and competitive loan products. They also provide ancillary services such as wire transfers (domestic and international), check ordering, notary services, photocopying, fax services, and coin/currency exchange. The institution emphasizes 24-hour access through online and mobile platforms alongside traditional lobby and drive-up services.

The First National Bank of Proctor distinguishes itself primarily through its community-focused approach and local decision-making. Leadership explicitly highlights that decisions are made locally rather than at a distant corporate headquarters, and employees are noted as being actively involved in church offices, booster clubs, and community activities. This positioning reflects a traditional community bank model where personalized service and local relationships take precedence.

As a very small bank with only $25 million in assets and a single location, this institution is best suited for Proctor-area residents and commuters seeking relationship banking and local accessibility. Prospective customers should be aware of the limited branch network, modest operating hours (closed Saturdays and Sundays, with abbreviated weekday hours Tuesday-Thursday), and the constraints that come with a small institution. Those seeking extensive branch networks, 24/7 in-person service, or specialized financial products may find better options at larger regional or national banks.
services: Mobile banking app with deposit capture
Online banking with bill pay capability
Competitive loan products (details not specified on available pages)
Safe deposit boxes in various sizes
pros: Independent bank with local decision-making authority, not subject to distant corporate policies
Long-established institution serving the community since 1902, indicating stability and local trust
Mobile banking app and online banking available for 24-hour account access
Comprehensive service offerings including wire transfers, safe deposit boxes, notary services, and check ordering
Personal relationship banking with staff actively involved in community activities
Competitive loan and deposit rates explicitly stated as a core offering
Drive-up services available for convenient transaction processing
cons: Very small asset base ($25 million) and single-location operation limits scalability and service expansion
Limited operating hours with lobby closed before 5 PM Tuesday-Thursday and completely closed weekends
Small staff (8 employees) may result in slower service during peak times or limited specialized expertise
No mention of FDIC insurance specifics, though standard banking disclosures appear limited on website
Geographic limitation—location in Proctor, Minnesota makes this impractical for non-local customers
best_for: Proctor, Minnesota residents seeking relationship-based banking with local decision-making
Small business owners and community members wanting to support a locally-owned financial institution
Customers prioritizing personal service and direct staff relationships over extensive branch networks
Those needing basic banking services with moderate lending products in a small-town setting
diagnosis: The First National Bank of Proctor is best for Proctor-area residents and local business owners who prioritize relationship banking and community connection over service convenience and product breadth. The primary caveat is that this is a very small, single-location bank with limited hours and geographic reach—it is not suitable for customers seeking extensive branch networks, weekend banking, or specialized financial products.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
